name: Baan Rim Talay Local Kitchen
reason: Family-run seafood by the pier, locally owned, 8 min away. Tourists love the crab curry.
whyLocal: Owned by a Rawai fishing family for 20+ years; sources catch from the morning pier auction.
note: Cash preferred. Gets busy after 19:00 — share early or call ahead for groups.
sum: Consistently praised for fresh seafood, fair prices and friendly owners.

name: Old Town Herbal Massage
reason: Certified Thai therapists in a restored Sino-Portuguese shophouse, 4.8★. Great for a rainy afternoon.
whyLocal: Run by a local therapist cooperative; uses herbal compresses made in-house.
note: Booking recommended on weekends. Upstairs — not step-free.
sum: Clean, professional, authentic — repeatedly called the best value massage in Old Town.

name: Rawai Fisherfolk Experience
reason: Half-day with a local sea-gypsy community — net fishing, cooking, fair-priced and genuinely local.
whyLocal: Community-based tourism run by the Urak Lawoi people; income stays in the village.
note: Booking required a day ahead. Bring sun protection. Light walking on the beach.
sum: Travelers describe it as the most authentic, respectful local experience on the island.

name: Kathu Local Dessert Café
reason: Traditional Phuket desserts and local coffee in an old tin-mining quarter. Quiet, cheap, charming.
whyLocal: Family recipes from Kathu's mining heritage; ingredients from the local market.
note: Closes at 17:00 — best for morning or early afternoon.
sum: A local favourite for nostalgic Phuket sweets and a calm break off the tourist trail.

name: Phuket Craft House
reason: Hand-made batik, ceramics and pearl jewellery from island artisans — real souvenirs, not factory goods.
whyLocal: Stocks only Phuket-based makers; each piece is tagged with the maker's story.
note: Card accepted. Can arrange shipping for larger items.
sum: Shoppers value the authenticity and the direct support to local craftspeople.

name: Nai Yang Coconut Workshop
reason: Hands-on coconut craft & local snacks near the airport beach — affordable, great for families.
whyLocal: Smallholder family farm; teaches traditional coconut processing.
note: Verification in progress — English support is limited. Confirm slot on LINE.
sum: Early reviews are warm; a quiet, hands-on stop for travellers near Nai Yang.
